reviewer: Claude session acting as head coach over the 3-seat panel (NOT the 6 AM automatic run)
brief: Legs B. Three trainers reviewed you separately tonight and split on three lifts — each card says who won and why. Headline: the RDL does NOT get restructured. Progression wanted to bin the 155 warm-up and give you three sets at 205; recovery showed your rest before the top set stretched to about three and a half minutes last time and the last set still came back at 7, four days after pulling 255 for triples, at 206 lb — ten under your winter weight. Recovery won: same ramp as last week, and the job is eight honest reps on BOTH top sets. Because the RDL stayed light, I gave progression the leg curl: all four sets at 135, and that fourth set is the whole point — it has gone unlogged three sessions running and it is the only thing standing between you and 142.5. Leg extension holds at 142.5 but chase 14/13/13; the weight is unchanged so there is no added risk, and if the reps fade like they did Saturday, stop. Calves lose the 200 opener and gain a real fourth set: 240, two at 280, then 260 for 15. Cable crunch stays 50.5 and the earn is reps, not pins — you have logged exactly 12 on every set for two weeks and the range runs to 15. The program seat also flagged four structural problems for you to approve or reject; they are in the packet and none of them touched tonight's numbers.

flags.note: Third training day in a row and bodyweight is 206, about ten under winter — recovery held the RDL for that reason.
The fourth set of leg curls and calf presses has gone unlogged for weeks. Tonight that fourth set IS the progression.

dissent.progression: 185/205/205/205 — the 155 was a warm-up eating hamstring volume
dissent.recovery: 155/185/205/205 — rest drifted to 3.5 min and the last set came back 7
dissent.why: Progression is right that the ramp wastes a set, but that is a structural fix, not tonight's. Asking for three sets at 205 when he could not complete two, four days after heavy deadlifts and ten pounds down on bodyweight, buys another failed rep. Fix the ramp in the plan, not on a night he is already carrying fatigue.

dissent.progression: 135 x 4 — the slot says four sets and three were logged; the fourth set is the progression
dissent.recovery: 135 x 3 — hamstrings already fed by RDLs, deadlifts and Saturday's curls
dissent.why: Recovery priced this against progression's HEAVIER RDL, which I just refused. With the RDL holding at last week's ramp the hamstring cost of tonight is lower than either seat assumed, so the fourth set is affordable — and it is the exact set whose absence has blocked this lift for three sessions.

dissent.progression: 142.5 x 14/13/13 — four exposures at this weight and never once at the top of the range
dissent.recovery: 142.5 x 12/12/12 — quads faded inside the session on Saturday, third straight training day
dissent.why: Both seats want the same weight, so nothing is added to the load — the only question is what he chases. Prescribing the 12 he already does guarantees the trigger never fires. Recovery's fatigue read is real and is why the weight does not move; if the reps fade, stop short and it still counts.

overrides.reason: Same ramp as last week on purpose — your rest stretched to three and a half minutes before the top set and it still came back 7. Eight honest reps on both top sets is the job.
Your 120 opener sat 40 lb under your working weight — that is a ramp, not a set. 140 counts, then 11s on both at 160.
Four sets at 135. The fourth set has gone unlogged three sessions running and it is the only thing between you and 142.5 — tonight it IS the progression.
Weight holds at 142.5 — you have handled it four times and never touched the top of the range. Chase 14/13/13; if the reps fade like Saturday, stop short.

The 200 opener comes off the card — you did 260x15 twice on Saturday. 240, two at 280, then 260 for 15 to close: that is the fourth set you have been skipping, and it is the cheapest volume in the session.
50.5 is what you already handled Saturday. You have logged exactly 12 reps on every set for two weeks and the range runs to 15 — tonight the earn is reps, not pins.

proposals.why: The PushA face-pull slot is a ghost — last logged there 2026-02-24. You run face pulls live on PullA, PushB and PullB.
The plan asks 4 sets on both leg days; you have never hit the fourth. Cut the prescription to 3 real sets or commit to the 4th.
Same lift, two days, 15 lb and a set apart — on LegsA it sits behind leg press and extensions and gets the tired end of the session.
Six weekly sets of extension against five of curl, on top of leg press both days. Front of the leg is out-earning the back.
PushA calls the heavier range but you press heavier on PushB — the two prescriptions are backwards relative to where you are fresh.
It lands at the tail of a long session and reads as clock-punching rather than core work.
